verification_note: git rev-parse HEAD matches pinned_sha exactly; working tree porcelain shows only ?? CODEBASE_AUDIT.md and ?? audit/ (both expected, pre-existing/self-generated), no drift.

configured.pytest_config: [tool.pytest.ini_options]: testpaths=["tests"], asyncio_mode="auto", markers=["integration: tests that spawn real browsers"]
configured.coverage_config: [tool.coverage.run] source=["src/stealth_chrome_devtools_mcp"]; [tool.coverage.report] show_missing=true. NOTE: no fail_under key in pyproject.toml.
configured.dependency_pinning: uv.lock tracked; direct deps in pyproject.toml are mostly exact-pinned (==), one range (requests>=2.32.0,<4)
configured.gitignore_tool_hints: .gitignore lists .ruff_cache/ and .mypy_cache/ even though neither tool is configured anywhere

enforced_in_ci.test.yml/unit-tests: matrix py3.11/3.12/3.13; `uv sync --extra test` then `uv run pytest -m "not integration" -v --tb=short --cov=src/stealth_chrome_devtools_mcp --cov-report=term-missing --cov-fail-under=39`
enforced_in_ci.test.yml/integration-tests: py3.12 only, needs unit-tests; installs real Chrome+Xvfb; `uv run pytest -m integration -v --tb=short --timeout=120` (no coverage flag)
enforced_in_ci.publish.yml/test: matrix py3.11/3.12/3.13; `uv run pytest -m "not integration" -v --tb=short` (no coverage flag) as a gate before build+publish
enforced_in_ci.publish.yml/publish: `uv build` + pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish (pinned SHA) + changelog/GH-release generation; all third-party actions are SHA-pinned (not tag-pinned)

gaps: coverage --cov-fail-under=39 exists only as a CI CLI flag (test.yml); pyproject.toml [tool.coverage.report] has no fail_under, so local `pytest --cov` runs never enforce the gate (enforced-in-ci, not-configured-in-pyproject)
.gitignore pre-declares .ruff_cache/ and .mypy_cache/ but neither ruff nor mypy has any config in pyproject.toml and neither runs in any workflow (anticipated-but-never-wired-up)

no linter configured or enforced anywhere; unconfigured `ruff check` probe found 99 violations (67 unused-import, 15 f-string-missing-placeholders, 7 unused-variable, 6 module-import-not-at-top, 3 ambiguous-variable-name, 1 undefined-name/F821 -- correctness-adjacent, not just style)
no type-checker (mypy/pyright) configured or enforced anywhere
no formatter (black / ruff format) configured or enforced anywhere
no dependency-audit / vulnerability scanning configured or enforced anywhere (no CI step, no dependabot.yml); ad-hoc pip-audit run for this stage found 34 known vulnerabilities across 9 packages
publish.yml's pre-publish test gate omits --cov-fail-under entirely, so a release can ship on a build that push/PR CI would have blocked on coverage
integration tests run on a single Python version (3.12) while unit tests run the full 3.11/3.12/3.13 matrix -- asymmetric version coverage

loc.note: tracked .py files only (git ls-files), src concentration: server.py alone is 4207 LOC = 27.6% of src/ and 19.6% of all tracked .py in the repo

churn_top.method: git log --format= --name-only, counted per tracked filename occurrence, deleted/renamed-away files excluded

complexity_worst.tool: radon cc -s -n D src/ (via uvx, grade D-or-worse only)
complexity_worst.note: proxy_forwarder.py and dynamic_hook_system.py are complexity hotspots absent from both the largest-files and churn-top lists -- stable-but-complex, a different risk shape than server.py.

dep_audit.tool: uv run --with pip-audit pip-audit (ephemeral overlay in the project's real resolved environment; a bare `uvx pip-audit` would only audit its own isolated env, not the project, so it was rejected)
